zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Isabel Graham於1176年出生在英國蘇格蘭艾斯爾時，她的父親John de Graham50歲，母親Lady Graham46歲。她於大約1200年與Sir David Muir of Polkelly在英國蘇格蘭艾斯爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1296年在蘇格蘭艾斯爾艾爾享年120歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Isabel Graham于1176年出生在英国苏格兰艾斯爾时，她的父亲John de Graham50岁，母亲Lady Graham46岁。她于大约1200年与Sir David Muir of Polkelly在英国苏格兰艾斯爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1296年在苏格兰艾斯爾艾爾享年120岁。

nameMeanings.name: Graham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Grantham in Lincolnshire, recorded in Domesday Book as Graham (as well as Grantham, Grandham, and Granham). See also Grantham .

Irish (especially Ulster): in Ireland Graham is generally the Scottish name, but it has additionally been adopted as an Anglicized form of both Ó Creacháin (see Crehan ) and Ó Gormghaile (see Gormley ).

History: The surname Graham was taken to Scotland at the beginning of the 12th century by the Norman baron William de Graham, holder of the manor of Grantham in Lincolnshire, from whom many if not all modern bearers are probably descended. The chief of Clan Graham is the Duke of Montrose.

variants.name: Greim
meanings: South German: from a short form of a compound personal name beginning with the element grīm ‘mask, helmet’.
Scottish: variant of   Graham  .

variants.name: Grahm
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire and northern England): shortened form of   Graham  .

variants.name: Grantham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and East Yorkshire): habitational name from Grantham in Lincolnshire, of uncertain origin. The final element is Old English hām ‘homestead’; the first may be Old English grand ‘gravel’ or perhaps a personal name Granta, which probably originated as a byname meaning ‘snarler’. See also Graham .

variants.name: Grahame
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Graham  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 261, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘Morning dew’; also ‘generosity’.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Isabel Graham was born in 1176, in Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, her father, John de Graham, was 50 and her mother, Lady Graham, was 46. She married Sir David Muir of Polkelly about 1200, in Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1296, in Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland, at the age of 120.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Isabel Graham est née en 1176, à Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de Graham, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Lady Graham, avait 46 ans. Elle a épousé Sir David Muir of Polkelly environ 1200, à Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1296, à Ayr, Ayrshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 120 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Isabel Graham 1176 in Ayrshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Graham 50 und ihre Mutter Lady Graham 46. Sie hat um 1200 in Ayrshire, Schottland, United Kingdom Sir David Muir of Polkelly geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1296 im Alter von 120 Jahren in Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Isabel Graham è nata il nel 1176 nel Ayrshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, sua padre, John de Graham, aveva 50 anni e sua madre, Lady Graham, aveva 46 anni. Lei sposato Sir David Muir of Polkelly circa 1200, nel Ayrshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1296, nel Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland, all'età di 120 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Isabel Graham(이)가 1176년 영국 스코틀랜드 에이르실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Graham(은)는 50세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Graham(은)는 46세였다. 그녀는 대략 1200년 영국 스코틀랜드 에이르실르에서 Sir David Muir of Polkelly(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1296년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 에이르에서 120세의 나이로 사망했다.
